disclaimer: Educational assessment. CARSI is an IICRC-approved CEC provider; this is CARSI-original Australian training, not IICRC certification.
note: Australian-produced. Grounded in the course's own modules. Answer keys independently verified against the course text. Founder to review before wiring live.

questions.questionText: How are the HydraMaster and Sapphire Scientific petrol-engine truck-mounts in this course powered?
options: An onboard petrol engine drives the pump and blower, and the onboard electrics are 12 V DC - not 240 V mains
They plug into 240 V mains power at the customer premises
An onboard converter changes US 120 V into Australian 240 V on the machine
A separate diesel generator carried by the operator supplies mains power

questions.questionText: What is the correct order for the truck-mount startup sequence?
options: Engine first, then pressure, then water last
Pressure first, then water, then the engine
Water on first, then the engine, then pressure
Chemical prime first, then water and pressure together

questions.questionText: When the carbon monoxide monitor alarms at 35 ppm, what is the required response?
options: Open the van doors and keep cleaning until the job is finished
Evacuate to fresh air, shut the engine down, and notify the supervisor
Wait until symptoms such as headache appear before acting
Nudge the exhaust to one side and continue working

questions.questionText: What is the Australian workplace exposure standard for carbon monoxide taught in this course?
options: 200 ppm as an 8-hour average
6,000 ppm as an 8-hour average
140 ppm with no scheduled change
30 ppm as an 8-hour average, reducing to 20 ppm effective 2026

questions.questionText: What is the minimum rating the high-pressure solution hose must meet?
options: At least 3,000 psi and at least 121 C - plain thermoplastic hose must never be used
Any garden hose is acceptable for the solution line
Only 500 psi and 40 C are required
Plain thermoplastic hose rated to 1,000 psi is sufficient

questions.questionText: How does Australian law split obligations for the host vehicle by Gross Vehicle Mass (GVM)?
options: Any vehicle over 2 t is a heavy vehicle
GVM has no bearing on which rules apply
4.5 t GVM or less is a light vehicle; over 4.5 t is a heavy vehicle regulated by the NHVR
All slide-in vans are classed as heavy vehicles

questions.questionText: How must recovery (waste) water from the truck-mount be disposed of?
options: It is domestic greywater and may go into any street stormwater drain
It is commercial trade waste and must go to an approved sewer point under a trade-waste permit, never to stormwater
It may be tipped onto the ground or a garden at the job site
It may be discharged into the nearest waterway
questions.sourceRef: Unit 3.3 - Storage, Transport and Road Safety

questions.questionText: What forward performance standard must the load restraint on a mounted unit meet?
options: There is no performance standard for restraint
The load must resist only 0.1 g forward
The load must resist 5 g forward
The restraint must hold the load under heavy braking, resisting at minimum 0.8 g forward
questions.sourceRef: Unit 3.3 - Storage, Transport and Road Safety

questions.questionText: What is the rule on chemistry used to descale the heat exchanger?
options: Use only the manufacturers descaler; never use concentrated acids or solvents including d-limonene in the water or chemical system
Any strong household acid at a guessed dilution works well
d-limonene is the preferred descaling agent for the heat exchanger
Descaling is unnecessary because Australian water is always soft

questions.questionText: How is the carbon monoxide control criterion treated in the operator competency assessment?
options: A carbon monoxide shortfall lowers the score but the operator can still pass overall
Carbon monoxide is not part of the competency assessment
Any shortfall on the carbon monoxide control criterion is an automatic fail of the whole assessment
Carbon monoxide only matters when the host vehicle is a heavy vehicle
